methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $500M - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Fortem Technologies is a Series C–stage defense and security company that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies, including 8 private firms (Hidden Level, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and others) and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy, applied a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to the $100M base, yielding $170M. Peer-median analysis examined 10 defense & security peers across Series B and D stages, capturing a wide range from $500M to $9.2B, with a median valuation of $1.4B. This dispersion reflects meaningful stage and maturity variation within the comp set.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $144M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $772M. The wide spread reflects structural uncertainty in the comp set: the peer median of $1.4B is driven substantially by later-stage firms (Saronic at $9.2B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B), while the stage-progression method anchors to current capital deployed. Key uncertainties include Fortem's revenue scale, customer concentration in federal procurement, and competitive positioning within the crowded defense-tech landscape. A Series C round would likely clarify commercial traction and narrow this range materially.
